posts_last_12h.author: Dan F Jim
posts_last_12h.text:
Making the most of the wind season here in WA. Now looking for some storm winds In the next few months! Loving wing foiling in the sawn river!

Caption: Catching some air on the Swan River with my trusty Axis and Duotone gear! #Wingfoiling #swanriver #australia #axis #duotone
posts_last_12h.photo: Instagram cross-post, wing foiling on Swan River, WA Australia

posts_last_12h.text: They latest race, surf and dock Axis gear with international team rider Kai Thompson

linked_content.title: What's new at Axis with Kai Thompson

posts_last_12h.author: Vangelis Evan Mavridoglou
posts_last_12h.text:
We surveyed nearly 150 AXIS riders across 15+ countries to find out what they're riding, how they set it up, and what they'd change.
The results are in, and some of them surprised us.
Want your setup counted? Takes 3 minutes.
posts_last_12h.note: Same content also posted by AXIS Foils official page 22h ago

posts_last_24h_older.author: Dmitry Evseev
posts_last_24h_older.text:
Comparison of Axis foils. Which one is which?
Answer options (pls answer in comment):
Tempo 1090 | Fireball 1070

key_comments.author: Maxime Meyer
key_comments.text: Fun game! I would say: Fireball 1070 on the left, Tempo 1090 on the right. Same wingspan more or less, but Tempo is less draggy, which leads to less BPM overall.

posts_last_24h_older.author: Darren Zielonka
posts_last_24h_older.text:
Mast Size Question
Does anyone prefer the 820mm over the 750mm for behind the boat or dock start? Pro?/cons?
I'm looking for a power carbon mast and thinking 820mm to wakefoil, wingfoil, and dock start. I only want to buy one haha.

key_comments.author: Perry Hughes
key_comments.text: I love my 820 for dock and wing, never use my old 750 anymore.

key_comments.author: Reuben Williams
key_comments.text: If you Wingfoil in rough seas or waves, the extra length is always useful for breach prevention, 82 is my sweet spot for wing, surf, dock, tow.

posts_last_24h_older.author: Paul Schnetker
posts_last_24h_older.text: I am looking at buying a surge (or spitfire) on the Gold Coast Australia. I am 85kg, intermediate fliteboad L2 efoil rider (Axis wing adaptor) just starting to ride waves (rode 18 little rides last session). Any recommendations on size? Not sure how small I would enjoy and don't have funds for multiple wings.

key_comments.author: Mark Madden
key_comments.text: I have a 1100 spit fire glides great on small waves I'm moving to armstrong so make me a stupid offer

key_comments.author: Sean Richardson
key_comments.text: I'm 85kg live on the Gold Coast as well. I have most of the Surges. If it was just one - for me I'd go the 830 - ride mostly at the Alley, winging in waves. Check in with SurfFx - they have demos… take out the guesswork

posts_last_24h_older.author: Tom La Majorette
posts_last_24h_older.text: Hi, I'm looking for advice for a new front [See more]

key_comments.author: Sean Richardson
key_comments.text: Robert Sellar dock starts the Surge I think - he might have some ideas. I wingfoil in waves and it's the Surge all the way. Have no fear of short chord, high aspect - these wings lift like no other. I wing in the 830 in 13-14knots

key_comments.author: Steven Fisher
key_comments.text: Do you want one foil to do all those disciplines? I would say Fireball for Parawinging and Dock and a Surge for prone. Your tail matters too. Go with a Surf Skinny for the tail.

posts_last_24h_older.author: Niels van der Kaaij
posts_last_24h_older.text:
Shout out to my old HPS 930 with the 380 speed stab.
Have not used this combi for almost a year and just a few days ago we got thermal side off wind 11 to 17 knots. Very patchy and due to low sea water temp (8°c) priorotizing fast on foil over glide. Amazing to experience how little is needed with a 5.0 wing and 92 liter board with rider weight of 91 KG in full wintersuit. While I have been using my most recent purchase, the ART V2 879 the HPS feels like I am on a full...

key_comments.author: Ian Sloan
key_comments.text: HPS 930 is a great foil. It was my go to for like 2 years. Spitfires have that same easy feeling.
